title: Xanthoxalis micrantha
taxonomy: Xanthoxalis micrantha is a species of flowering plant in the family Oxalidaceae. It is native to North America and is also known as the small wood-sorrel. The classification of Xanthoxalis micrantha includes its genus, Xanthoxalis, which is often considered a synonym of Oxalis. Within the Oxalidaceae family, Xanthoxalis micrantha is part of the order Rosales. The plant's classification is also sometimes recognized at the species level in the APG III system.
uses: Xanthoxalis micrantha is used in traditional medicine due to its purported health benefits. The plant has been employed to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. Its leaves are often used to make teas or infusions, which are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Additionally, Xanthoxalis micrantha is sometimes used as a natural remedy for skin conditions and wounds. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal applications and potential side effects.

intro: Xanthoxalis micrantha, also known as yellow wood sorrel, is a perennial plant species within the Oxalidaceae family, characterized by its yellow flowers and edible leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including treating gastrointestinal issues, reducing inflammation, and serving as a natural remedy for scurvy due to its high vitamin C content. The bioactive constituents of Xanthoxalis micrantha include oxalic acid, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Xanthoxalis micrantha is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a popular ingredient in folk medicine and natural remedies.
